task_completion.description: Agent logged into the OpenMRS system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS dashboard or administration page is visible, indicating successful authentication with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the Manage Roles section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Roles' page in OpenMRS is visible, showing a list of existing roles.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the 'Midwife' role for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit page for the 'Midwife' role is visible. The role name 'Midwife' should be visible in the form.
The 'Add Patients' privilege is checked or selected in the list of available privileges for the Midwife role.
A success message (e.g., 'Role saved successfully') is visible on the screen, or the browser has returned to the Manage Roles list after clicking save.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with OpenMRS.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions are performed in a web browser interface, not via terminal commands like curl or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual OpenMRS web interface (https://localhost/openmrs/admin), not a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success message.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open to manipulate the DOM for a fake success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target role to edit is 'Midwife' (description: 'Midwifery staff responsible for maternity care'). The target privilege to add is 'Add Patients'.
